facebook_caption:
Weekend flight delayed or cancelled from the UK or EU?

You may be able to claim up to £520 / €600 per passenger under UK/EU passenger rights rules.

FlightRefundClaim offers a free eligibility check and works on a no win, no fee basis.

Check your claim here:
https://flightrefundclaim.co.uk/?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=uk_weekly_visibility&utm_content=week1_sun_delay

#FlightDelay #FlightCompensation #UK261 #EU261 #TravelHelp

facebook_caption:
Delayed on a work trip or while travelling from a UK/EU airport?

If your flight arrived more than 3 hours late, you may have a claim under UK261 or EU261 rules.

Start with a free eligibility check:
https://flightrefundclaim.co.uk/flight-delay-compensation-uk/?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=uk_weekly_visibility&utm_content=week1_mon_business

facebook_caption:
Was your flight 3+ hours late arriving?

Under UK261/EU261 rules, some passengers can claim compensation when the airline was responsible for the disruption.

Check your eligibility here:
https://flightrefundclaim.co.uk/uk261-compensation-claim/?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=uk_weekly_visibility&utm_content=week1_tue_three_hours

facebook_caption:
Travel tip: if your flight is delayed or cancelled, keep your boarding pass, booking reference, and airline messages.

Those details can help when checking a compensation claim.

Free eligibility check:
https://flightrefundclaim.co.uk/?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=uk_weekly_visibility&utm_content=week1_sat_boarding_pass
